title: Collapse three-level nested if into a single `with` chain
prompt:
Given:

```
def deliver(params) do
  if valid?(params) do
    case lookup_user(params.id) do
      {:ok, user} ->
        if can_mail?(user) do
          MyApp.Mailer.send(user, params.body)
        else
          {:error, :opted_out}
        end
      :not_found -> {:error, :not_found}
    end
  else
    {:error, :invalid}
  end
end
```

Rewrite as a `with` expression. Helper functions: `valid?/1`, `lookup_user/1`, `can_mail?/1`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Delivery do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Three control-structure styles (if, case, if) to unify into `with`. Requires wrapping boolean helpers into `{:ok, _}`-shaped calls.
